contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 75
- Description: TrueSight Autopilot architecture proposal + DeepSeek CLI workstation setup. (1) Read truesight_autopilot codebase, agentic_ai_context, the 2026-05-03 credential audit note, and the post-merge state to assess viability of using the Claude Agent SDK for the Tier 3 fix loop. (2) Drafted a 640-line proposal at docs/PROPOSAL_claude_agent_sdk_fix_loop.md covering: vendor proof with PyPI/GitHub/docs URLs (the implementer Kimi had been disputing the SDK exists), 3-tier cost discipline that keeps DeepSeek as Tier 2 classifier and only routes proven-actionable failures through Claude at Tier 3 (~5 USD/day worst-case at MAX_PR_PER_DAY=5), concrete file plan with new app/fix_agent.py + app/sandbox.py + app/safety_hooks.py and a leave-alone list that explicitly accommodates Kimi's recent governor_chatbot_service merge, skeleton code for the SDK wrapper + throwaway-clone worktree isolation + PreToolUse hooks that hard-block git push --force / --no-verify / rm -rf / sudo / curl-pipe-bash / writes outside sandbox, inline SDK API reference (query vs ClaudeSDKClient, ClaudeAgentOptions fields, permission_mode values, HookMatcher signature), implementation order with a deliberately-broken-fixture-repo integration test, and credential prerequisites tied to the existing audit. (3) Recovered from parallel Kimi session clobbering my unstaged work mid-flight (Kimi committed 7a68fc1 governor merge while I was working) by switching to a git worktree at /tmp/autopilot-proposal, rewriting the proposal there, rebasing onto Kimi's new main, force-with-lease pushing, and opening PR #1 left intentionally unmerged for Kimi to read against. Reinforced the existing memory rule about using worktrees during parallel agent sessions. (4) Set up OpenCode TUI on the workstation: installed opencode-ai 1.14.33 via npm, wrote ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json registering DeepSeek as an OpenAI-compatible provider with deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner models, populated ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json from DEEPSEEK_SDK in truesight_autopilot/.env without plaintext-echoing the secret, chmod 600 the auth file, smoke-tested with opencode run. User confirmed it's working nicely. Gives the user a Claude-Code-style TUI driving DeepSeek as a peer to the existing claude / kimi installs.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
--------

My Digital Signature: 

Request Transaction ID: 

This submission was generated using https://dapp.truesight.me/create_signature.html

Verify submission here: https://dapp.truesight.me/verify_request.html
rubric: 100TDG For every 1 hour of human effort
contribution_type: 100TDG For every 1 hour of human effort
